description_long: This object is a blocky, stylized representation of a commercial passenger airplane, featuring a long, rectangular fuselage with a row of small, evenly spaced windows along each side. The wings are straight and extend outward from the midsection of the fuselage, with a slight upward dihedral angle and squared-off wingtips. The tail section includes a vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail design. The landing gear is visible beneath the fuselage, with two main gear units and a nose gear, all rendered in a simplified, blocky style. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage, distinguished by a slightly raised section and a few dark blocks representing cockpit windows. The overall color scheme is primarily light gray with some darker accents on the windows, landing gear, and tail.
description: A blocky, light gray commercial airplane with straight wings, a conventional tail, and visible landing gear. The fuselage features rows of windows and a distinct cockpit at the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetrical extension of the wings and landing gear.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, row of windows, straight wings, and the tail assembly.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, the arrangement of the tail, and the overall blocky structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the other side of the fuselage, wings, and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight, slightly dihedral wings and squared wingtips; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engines present on the wings or fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all fixed and non-retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit at the front of the fuselage with multiple window blocks.
